The metre is

Indications at Dawn and Twilight XXX 293
fqf?rT ?mt ^ ^cn
fq^m: Hcmgn^r ?m: n^u
Characteristics of a dawn produce their effects—good or bad—
at the same moment (i.e., immediately); of the evening, on the same
night or in three days; halos, dust and cross-bar of clouds, in a week
if not the same dayj the Sun-beams {Amogka and the like), rainbow,
lightning, mock-sun, clouds and wind act in the same manner; birds
produce the effects on the 8th day, if not the same day; and beasts,
in a week.
[The metre is 5rr^Tfq^fcr.]

yojana with its gleam; lightning illumines siKyojanas with its flash;
